title: 10-Year Treasury Yield (DGS10) Daily Snapshot
summary: The 10-Year Treasury Yield closed at 4.29% on April 9, 2026, unchanged from the prior day. The yield has declined 6 basis points over the past week, falling from 4.35% on April 3 to the current level.
key_points: 10-Year Treasury Yield stood at 4.29% as of 2026-04-09
The yield was unchanged on a day-over-day basis (4.29% on April 8 to 4.29% on April 9)
Weekly decline: 4.35% (April 3) to 4.29% (April 9), a 6 basis point decrease
The yield trended lower across the week: 4.35% → 4.34% → 4.33% → 4.29% → 4.29%
why_it_matters: The 10-year Treasury yield is a benchmark for mortgage rates, corporate borrowing costs, and risk-free asset valuations. This snapshot indicates the yield has stabilized near 4.29% after declining modestly from 4.35% earlier in the week.
macro_context: The 10-year yield around 4.29% suggests ongoing normalization from post-election highs, consistent with moderating inflation expectations and steady Fed policy positioning. No specific macro catalyst is identified in this data snapshot.
